word: G2398 - ἴδιος - ídios - id'-ee-os - His acquaintance

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἴδιος

- Transliteration: Idios

- Phonetic: id'-ee-os

- Definition:

1. pertaining to one's self, one's own, belonging to one's self

- Origin: of uncertain affinity

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Adjective

- Strong's: Of uncertain affinity; pertaining to self that is one's own; by implication private or separate: - X his acquaintance when they were alone apart aside due his (own proper several) home (her our thine your) own (business) private (-ly) proper severally their (own).
